news.title: Personalized vaccine shows promise against aggressive brain cancer
news.summary_en: A WashU Medicine-led clinical trial has found that a personalized vaccine to treat glioblastoma appears to increase recurrence-free survival in a subset of patients after surgery.

news.title: Study finds digital therapy app improves student mental health
news.summary_en: WashU researchers led a population-based study of thousands of college students showing that a phone app with text coaching increased access to care and eased symptoms of depression, anxiety and eating disorders.

news.title: How to build a discovery machine
news.summary_en: WashU McKelvey Engineering researchers have mapped out how to create artificial intelligence machines for solving problems with potentially trillions of complicating factors.

news.title: Scaling up the circular economy
news.summary_en: Researchers at WashU McKelvey Engineering are addressing the challenges of scaling up carbon dioxide electrolysis for industrial use.

news.title: New residence hall to be named James E. and Clara P. McLeod House
news.summary_en: The late James McLeod, vice chancellor for students and dean of the College of WashU Arts & Sciences, founded the Ervin Scholars Program and, along with his wife, Clara, challenged the WashU community to know each student “by name and by story.”

news.title: Arvin appointed executive vice chancellor for advancement
news.summary_en: Will Arvin, vice chancellor for medical advancement at Washington University in St. Louis, has been appointed executive vice chancellor for advancement, effective Jan. 5, according to Chancellor Andrew D. Martin. Arvin will succeed Pamella A. Henson, who is retiring.
